edge_cases.title: non-UTF-8 bytes in JSON file raise UnicodeDecodeError not JSONDecodeError
edge_cases.expected: _load_json_safely returns None without raising — UnicodeDecodeError must be caught
edge_cases.test_stub:
import tempfile, os
def test_load_json_safely_non_utf8_bytes(tmp_path):
    p = tmp_path / 'bad.json'
    p.write_bytes(b'\xff\xfe{\x00i\x00n\x00v\x00a\x00l\x00i\x00d\x00}')
    result = _load_json_safely(str(p))
    assert result is None

edge_cases.title: cap_hit boundary: iteration=3/max=3 does NOT trigger cap in evaluate_convergence
edge_cases.expected: cap_hit=False when iteration==max_iterations because code uses `>` not `>=`; must be pinned as intentional before any change
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_evaluate_convergence_cap_hit_exact_boundary():
    jsons = {'r': {'reviewer': 'r', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 3,
                   'verdict': 'needs_revision',
                   'findings': [{'severity': 'critical', 'category': 'bug',
                                 'file': 'f', 'finding': 'x'*8,
                                 'suggested_fix': 'y'*8}]}}
    result = evaluate_convergence(jsons, ['r'], iteration=3, max_iterations=3)
    assert result['cap_hit'] is False
    assert result['converged'] is False

edge_cases.title: evaluate_stage5_two_tier uses >= for cap while evaluate_convergence uses > — asymmetric boundary
edge_cases.surface: check_convergence.py / evaluate_stage5_two_tier line 364 vs evaluate_convergence line 135
edge_cases.expected: evaluate_stage5_two_tier with iteration=3/max=3 and tier-2 CRITICAL sets requires_user_override=True; evaluate_convergence with same inputs sets cap_hit=False

edge_cases.title: evaluate_stage5_dual CRITICAL must return action='escalate' not 'retry' — no retry budget at Stage 5
edge_cases.expected: Any CRITICAL finding in either agent returns action='escalate'; 'retry' is never a valid action for single-shot Stage 5
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_evaluate_stage5_dual_no_retry_on_critical():
    red_team = {'reviewer': 'red-team', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 1,
                'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'needs_revision',
                'findings': [{'severity': 'critical', 'category': 'security',
                              'file': 'f', 'finding': 'x'*8, 'suggested_fix': 'y'*8}]}
    architect = {'reviewer': 'architect', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 1,
                 'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve', 'findings': []}
    result = evaluate_stage5_dual(red_team, architect)
    assert result['final_converged'] is False
    assert result['action'] == 'escalate'
    assert result['action'] != 'retry'

edge_cases.title: evaluate_stage5_two_tier shim must not silently discard CRITICAL findings from non-selected tier-1 roles
edge_cases.expected: If any tier-1 role has a real CRITICAL, tier1_converged=False regardless of which role the shim collapses to
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_evaluate_stage5_two_tier_shim_preserves_tier1_criticals():
    tier1 = {
        'code-reviewer': {'reviewer': 'code-reviewer', 'schema_version': '1.0',
                          'iteration': 1, 'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'needs_revision',
                          'findings': [{'severity': 'critical', 'category': 'bug',
                                        'file': 'f', 'finding': 'x'*8,
                                        'suggested_fix': 'y'*8}]},
        'architect': {'reviewer': 'architect', 'schema_version': '1.0',
                      'iteration': 1, 'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve',
                      'findings': []}
    }
    import warnings
    with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True):
        result = evaluate_stage5_two_tier(tier1, None, iteration=1)
    assert result['tier1_converged'] is False

edge_cases.title: evaluate_stage5_two_tier deprecation warning must be emitted on every call
edge_cases.expected: Calling evaluate_stage5_two_tier emits DeprecationWarning; can be verified with warnings.catch_warnings
edge_cases.test_stub:
import warnings
def test_evaluate_stage5_two_tier_deprecation_warning():
    tier1 = {'architect': {'reviewer': 'architect', 'schema_version': '1.0',
                           'iteration': 1, 'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve',
                           'findings': []}}
    with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:
        warnings.simplefilter('always')
        evaluate_stage5_two_tier(tier1, None, iteration=1)
    assert any(issubclass(warning.category, DeprecationWarning) for warning in w)

edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_evaluate_convergence_iteration_zero():
    import pytest
    # If the implementation adds a guard:

    # If no guard is added (characterization), assert current behavior:

edge_cases.title: Phase 4 integration test ambiguous 'retry or escalate' assertion must be deterministic
edge_cases.expected: test_integration_stage3_round_2_with_carryover must be split into two tests with exact iteration values; no 'or' conditionals in assertions
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_integration_stage3_round_2_all_missing_retry(tmp_path):
    # Write round-1 files only; do NOT write round-2 files
    feature_dir = tmp_path / 'specs' / 'test-feature'
    (feature_dir / 'agent_verification').mkdir(parents=True)
    # (write round-1 files for round-1 pass, skip round-2)
    jsons = load_stage3_round_jsons(str(feature_dir), round_n=2,
                                    expected_roles=['architect', 'code-reviewer', 'tester'])
    result = evaluate_convergence(jsons, ['architect', 'code-reviewer', 'tester'],
                                  iteration=2, max_iterations=3)
    assert result['action'] == 'retry'

def test_integration_stage3_round_4_all_missing_escalate(tmp_path):
    feature_dir = tmp_path / 'specs' / 'test-feature'
    (feature_dir / 'agent_verification').mkdir(parents=True)
    jsons = load_stage3_round_jsons(str(feature_dir), round_n=4,
                                    expected_roles=['architect', 'code-reviewer', 'tester'])
    result = evaluate_convergence(jsons, ['architect', 'code-reviewer', 'tester'],
                                  iteration=4, max_iterations=3)
    assert result['action'] == 'escalate'

edge_cases.title: load_stage3_round_jsons with hyphenated role names: path construction must be verified with real role names
edge_cases.expected: round-1-code-reviewer.json, round-1-red-team.json resolved correctly; tests using single-word role names do not exercise this
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_load_stage3_round_jsons_hyphenated_roles(tmp_path):
    av = tmp_path / 'agent_verification'
    av.mkdir()
    import json
    payload = {'reviewer': 'code-reviewer', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 1,
               'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve', 'findings': []}
    (av / 'round-1-code-reviewer.json').write_text(json.dumps(payload))
    result = load_stage3_round_jsons(str(tmp_path), round_n=1,
                                     expected_roles=['code-reviewer', 'red-team'])
    assert result['code-reviewer'] is not None
    assert result['red-team'] is None

edge_cases.title: load_final_audit_jsons must not load tester_edge_cases.json as a reviewer artifact
edge_cases.expected: tester_edge_cases.json present in agent_verification/ is NOT returned by load_final_audit_jsons; only final_audit_<role>.json files are loaded
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_load_final_audit_jsons_ignores_carryover(tmp_path):
    av = tmp_path / 'agent_verification'
    av.mkdir()
    import json
    # Write a carryover file
    (av / 'tester_edge_cases.json').write_text(json.dumps({'schema_version': '1.0'}))
    # Write one real final audit file
    payload = {'reviewer': 'red-team', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 1,
               'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve', 'findings': []}
    (av / 'final_audit_red-team.json').write_text(json.dumps(payload))
    result = load_final_audit_jsons(str(tmp_path), expected_roles=['red-team', 'architect'])
    assert 'tester_edge_cases' not in result
    assert result['red-team'] is not None
    assert result['architect'] is None

edge_cases.title: feature_dir path with spaces or symlinks must not break path construction in loaders
edge_cases.expected: Path joins using os.path.join or pathlib.Path handle spaces and symlinks correctly; no string concatenation with / that would break on spaces
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_load_stage3_round_jsons_path_with_spaces(tmp_path):
    import json
    spaced = tmp_path / 'my feature dir'
    av = spaced / 'agent_verification'
    av.mkdir(parents=True)
    payload = {'reviewer': 'architect', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 1,
               'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve', 'findings': []}
    (av / 'round-1-architect.json').write_text(json.dumps(payload))
    result = load_stage3_round_jsons(str(spaced), round_n=1, expected_roles=['architect'])
    assert result['architect'] is not None

edge_cases.title: phase-end round-1 uses bare <role>.json path (no round-N prefix) — loader must not use round-1- prefix for round 1
edge_cases.expected: For phase_n=1, round_n=1: file is phase-1-verification/architect.json (no round prefix). For round_n=2: file is phase-1-verification/round-2-architect.json
edge_cases.test_stub:
def test_load_phase_end_round_jsons_round_1_no_prefix(tmp_path):
    import json
    pv = tmp_path / 'phase-1-verification'
    pv.mkdir()
    payload = {'reviewer': 'architect', 'schema_version': '1.0', 'iteration': 1,
               'status': 'ok', 'verdict': 'approve', 'findings': []}
    # Bare path, no round prefix
    (pv / 'architect.json').write_text(json.dumps(payload))
    # Also write the round-1 prefixed path to confirm it's NOT used
    (pv / 'round-1-architect.json').write_text(json.dumps({'wrong': True}))
    result = load_phase_end_round_jsons(str(tmp_path), phase_n=1, round_n=1,
                                        expected_roles=['architect'])
    assert result['architect']['reviewer'] == 'architect'
